examples.title: Algorithm: Cloud Resource Allocation
examples.description: Demonstrates how cloud providers allocate virtual resources from a resource pool to meet user demands
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CloudResourceAllocation
INPUT: userRequest, resourcePool, availableCapacity
OUTPUT: allocationResult

BEGIN
  // Initialize tracking variables
  SET allocatedResources = EMPTY
  SET remainingCapacity = availableCapacity
  
  // Check if sufficient resources are available
  FOR each resourceType IN userRequest.requiredResources
    IF remainingCapacity[resourceType] >= userRequest.amount[resourceType] THEN
      // Allocate resources from pool
      ALLOCATE userRequest.amount[resourceType] FROM resourcePool
      ADD resourceType TO allocatedResources
      UPDATE remainingCapacity[resourceType]
    ELSE
      // Insufficient resources - scale up or reject
      IF elasticityEnabled THEN
        SCALE_UP resources to meet demand
        ALLOCATE from new capacity
      ELSE
        REJECT request with "Insufficient Resources"
      END IF
    END IF
  END FOR
  
  // Create virtual machine instance
  IF allocationSuccessful THEN
    CREATE vmInstance WITH allocatedResources
    ASSIGN vmInstance TO userRequest
    RETURN success WITH vmInstance.details
  ELSE
    RETURN failure
  END IF
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the resource allocation algorithm used in cloud computing. It shows how a cloud provider checks available capacity, allocates resources from a pool, handles insufficient resources through elasticity, and creates virtual machine instances. This aligns with the resource pooling and on-demand self-service characteristics of cloud computing.

examples.title: Python: IaaS/PaaS/SaaS Service Model Demonstration
examples.description: Demonstrates the layered architecture of cloud service models with code
examples.code:
"""
Cloud Computing Service Models: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
Demonstrates the layered architecture and responsibilities in each model
"""

# Base class representing physical infrastructure
class PhysicalInfrastructure:
    """Represents the underlying physical hardware in data centers"""
    def __init__(self):
        self.servers = ["Server1", "Server2", "Server3"]
        self.storage = "10TB"
        self.network = "10Gbps"
    
    def get_info(self):
        return f"Physical: Servers={self.servers}, Storage={self.storage}, Network={self.network}"

# IaaS - Infrastructure as a Service
class IaaSProvider:
    """
    IaaS provides virtualized computing resources (VMs, storage, network)
    User manages: OS, middleware, runtime, data, applications
    Provider manages: physical infrastructure, virtualization
    """
    def __init__(self):
        self.physical = PhysicalInfrastructure()
        self.virtual_machines = []
    
    def create_vm(self, name, cpu, ram, os):
        """Create a virtual machine instance"""
        vm = {
            'name': name,
            'cpu': cpu,
            'ram': ram,
            'os': os,
            'status': 'running'
        }
        self.virtual_machines.append(vm)
        return f"VM '{name}' created with {cpu} CPU, {ram} RAM, OS: {os}"
    
    def list_vms(self):
        return self.virtual_machines

# PaaS - Platform as a Service
class PaaSProvider:
    """
    PaaS provides a development and deployment platform
    User manages: applications, data
    Provider manages: OS, middleware, runtime, physical infrastructure, virtualization
    """
    def __init__(self):
        self.iaas = IaaSProvider()
        self.platforms = []
    
    def deploy_application(self, app_name, runtime_env):
        """Deploy an application on the platform"""
        # Automatically creates VM with required runtime
        vm_name = f"{app_name}-vm"
        self.iaas.create_vm(vm_name, cpu=4, ram="8GB", os=runtime_env)
        
        platform = {
            'app_name': app_name,
            'runtime': runtime_env,
            'status': 'deployed'
        }
        self.platforms.append(platform)
        return f"Application '{app_name}' deployed on {runtime_env} platform"

# SaaS - Software as a Service
class SaaSProvider:
    """
    SaaS provides complete software applications over the internet
    User manages: minimal (user data, preferences)
    Provider manages: everything from physical infrastructure to application
    """
    def __init__(self):
        self.paas = PaaSProvider()
        self.applications = {}
    
    def provide_software(self, software_name, user_data):
        """Provide software as a service to end users"""
        # Automatically handles infrastructure and platform
        self.paas.deploy_application(software_name, "managed")
        
        self.applications[software_name] = {
            'users': [user_data],
            'status': 'active'
        }
        return f"Software '{software_name}' provided as service to {user_data}"

# Demonstration
if __name__ == "__main__":
    print("=" * 60)
    print("CLOUD SERVICE MODELS DEMONSTRATION")
    print("=" * 60)
    
    # IaaS Example - Amazon EC2 style
    print("\n--- IaaS: Infrastructure as a Service ---")
    iaas = IaaSProvider()
    print(iaas.physical.get_info())
    print(iaas.create_vm("web-server-1", cpu=2, ram="4GB", os="Ubuntu"))
    print(iaas.create_vm("db-server-1", cpu=4, ram="16GB", os="CentOS"))
    
    # PaaS Example - Google App Engine style  
    print("\n--- PaaS: Platform as a Service ---")
    paas = PaaSProvider()
    print(paas.deploy_application("my-webapp", "Python/Django"))
    print(paas.deploy_application("analytics-service", "Node.js"))
    
    # SaaS Example - Salesforce/Google Workspace style
    print("\n--- SaaS: Software as a Service ---")
    saas = SaaSProvider()
    print(saas.provide_software("CRM-System", "user@company.com"))
    print(saas.provide_software("Email-Service", "admin@company.com"))
    
    print("\n" + "=" * 60)
    print("COMPARISON SUMMARY:")
    print("=" * 60)
    print("IaaS: User manages OS & apps | Provider: Hardware & Virtualization")
    print("PaaS: User manages apps & data | Provider: OS, Runtime, Middleware")
    print("SaaS: User manages user data  | Provider: Complete application stack")
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates the three cloud service models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS) with a clear class hierarchy showing what each model provides versus what the user manages. It shows the progression from infrastructure-level control (IaaS) to fully managed software (SaaS), making the abstract concepts concrete and programmatically demonstrable.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of resources/instances

examples.title: C: Cloud Load Balancer Simulation
examples.description: Simulates how cloud load balancers distribute requests across multiple servers
examples.explanation: This C program simulates a cloud load balancer, demonstrating critical cloud computing concepts including resource pooling, load distribution across virtual machines, auto-scaling capabilities, and different load balancing algorithms (Round Robin and Least Connections). It shows how cloud systems distribute incoming requests across multiple servers to ensure efficient resource utilization and high availability.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for request processing where n is number of servers
